content.claim: Hungary centralized school governance and maintenance in 2013 under KLIK, then restructured again in 2017 to 58-60 school district centres, removing local municipal control over small-village school viability decisions and separating villages from ordinary municipal funding logic.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Describes governance structure but does not directly measure impact on school closure decisions
Does not establish whether centralized structure has improved or worsened equity outcomes in rural areas
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
